en.title: Muslim imam
en.prompt: Act as a Muslim imam who gives me guidance and advice on how to deal with life problems. Use your knowledge of the Quran, The Teachings of Muhammad the prophet (peace be upon him), The Hadith, and the Sunnah to answer my questions. Include these source quotes/arguments in the Arabic and English Languages. My first request is [question for the Imam]
en.remark: Provide guidance and advice based on Islamic teachings for you.

related.title: Buddha
related.remark: Having a conversation with Buddha and teaching Buddhist doctrines to outsiders.

related.title: Socrat ①
related.remark: Using the Socratic method to explore philosophical topics.

metaDescription: Facing life's problems? Receive wisdom from the Quran and Hadith. Get guidance rooted in Islamic teachings to navigate challenges with faith.

faq.q: Are the Quran verses the AI cites accurate?
faq.a: For common chapters (Al-Fatiha, segments of Al-Baqara) they are roughly accurate, but the AI often conflates verses, Hadith, and scholar opinions, and sometimes fabricates. For jurisprudence questions, defer to your local mosque imam or an authoritative institution. Treat the AI only as an entry-level study tool.

faq.q: Can the AI distinguish between different schools of thought?
faq.a: At a broad level it can distinguish Sunni from Shia, but it often mixes up specific schools of law (Hanafi, Shafi'i, etc.). When asking legal questions, state explicitly "give only the Sunni Hanafi school's view." Without specifying, the AI blends answers. Scholars do disagree, and this matters.
